claim: The 10,000-particle Li4SiO4 bed has a connected baseline pore skeleton.
boundary: DEM-derived voxel reconstruction only; not experimental CT.

claim: Increasing drag-to-weight ratio shifts debris downstream and increases weak breakthrough.
boundary: Representative states and current parameter window; one-way Stokes-scale forcing is audited in S18 but is not resolved pore-scale gas flow or a universal law.

claim: Weak breakthrough can occur without measurable pore-network clogging.
manuscript_location: Title; Abstract; Results; Discussion; Supplementary Figs. S20-S21
boundary: Connectivity is measured on the reconstructed bed skeleton; pressure calibration is not included.

claim: Time-resolved data show delayed leading-tail breakthrough after front migration.
manuscript_location: Results representative states; Supplementary Fig. S5

claim: Internal-source release remains retention dominated and rare-front formation is separated from bulk release over the analyzed windows.
manuscript_location: Results localized high-inventory release; Discussion; Supplementary Figs. S8-S10 and S19-S21
boundary: Three configured localized-release production rows reached their target times, including the 40 ms upstream case, the 10.05 ms downstream case and the 10.00 ms high-inventory case; still not a fitted source-position or source-strength law.

claim: Time-resolved tail-lag mining shows that the mobile leading tail, retained bulk and local blockage centre are separated state variables.
boundary: Derived from existing time-resolved and spatial-quantile tables; not an experimentally calibrated residence-time distribution, not a universal tailing law and not a critical-clogging transition.

claim: Axial deposition heterogeneity relaxes from an early localized filtration peak into broader downstream spreading while breakthrough remains weak.
boundary: Derived from existing axial numerical blockage profiles; not pore-scale bridge statistics, not experimental imaging and not a universal clogging law.

claim: Increasing debris inventory raises local blockage but remains in a pre-clogging regime.
manuscript_location: Results loading response; Fig. 5; Supplementary Figs. S20-S21
boundary: Three single-seed loading states; not a monotonic universal loading law.

claim: Ergun and scalar voxel-network pressure proxies predict small pressure/conductance changes, including bounded 3D resistance-amplification sensitivity.
manuscript_location: Methods pressure-free index; Results loading response; Supplement
boundary: Reduced proxy only; not OpenFOAM CFD, not LBM, not measured pressure and not pressure-calibrated Ib.

claim: Returned cropped-domain OpenFOAM pressure evidence is available for the three peak-blockage handoff cases.
boundary: Returned OpenFOAM pressure drops are cropped-domain numerical evidence only; they are not experimental pressure validation, not a full blanket-scale flow solution and not a pressure-calibrated safety limit.
action: Use bounded OpenFOAM pressure wording and keep the non-validation boundary explicit.

claim: The current manuscript does not establish a universal critical clogging transition.
boundary: Current scope is pre-clogging migration/filtering; no safety limit is validated.

claim: Journal submission is not ready until repository and author confirmations are complete.
boundary: Administrative blockers are not scientific evidence gaps, but they block actual submission.
action: Confirm DOI/accession, funding, competing interests, acknowledgements and corresponding author.
